audits.scope: stellar-core protocol implementation, consensus (SCP), cryptographic primitives, network layer
audits.summary: NCC Group reviewed stellar-core and found issues including a potential DoS via malformed XDR and minor cryptographic concerns, all addressed by SDF.

audits.scope: Stellar Consensus Protocol (SCP) formal verification and stellar-core implementation review
audits.summary: Kudelski reviewed SCP implementation correctness and found one high-severity logic edge case in quorum intersection handling that was subsequently patched.
audits.url: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — SDF references this engagement in blog posts but the full report PDF is not publicly hosted on GitHub or SDF docs as of research date; see https://stellar.org/blog/developers/security

audits.scope: Soroban smart contract runtime (WASM VM), host environment, and SDK security
audits.summary: Trail of Bits audited the Soroban smart contract platform pre-mainnet and identified sandbox escape risks and integer overflow vectors in the host environment, all remediated before launch.

exploit_history.description: Stellar inflation mechanism bug: a flaw in the inflation pool distribution logic allowed disproportionate XLM creation under specific voting conditions. Discovered internally and patched before large-scale exploitation; limited XLM minted incorrectly.

Fee-bump transaction edge case: a logic error in fee-bump transaction processing (CAP-0015) could cause nodes to diverge on transaction validity under high-load conditions. No funds lost; patched in stellar-core v12.1.

Horizon API (the public-facing REST layer) suffered a data inconsistency bug where ingestion could stall under reorg conditions, causing incorrect balance reads for ~2 hours. No funds lost; no consensus-layer impact.

dependencies.notes: Stellar's base layer has no on-chain oracle dependency; price feeds are not part of the core protocol. Soroban dApps may integrate third-party oracles but that is application-layer risk, not protocol-layer.

dependencies.provider: Stellar-Ethereum Bridge (various third-party: Allbridge, Squid, LOBSTR)
dependencies.notes: Cross-chain bridges to Stellar are third-party operated and carry standard bridge risk (custodial or semi-custodial). The Stellar protocol itself does not depend on any bridge for operation. Bridge exploits would not affect XLM holders who do not use them.
Stellar is an independent L1 using the Stellar Consensus Protocol (SCP), a federated Byzantine agreement variant. No dependency on Ethereum, Bitcoin, or any external L1 for liveness or safety.

rationale: Stellar's core protocol has a strong security track record with no catastrophic exploits in its 10+ year history; the SCP consensus mechanism has been formally analyzed and audited multiple times, and the SDF maintains an active HackerOne/Immunefi bug bounty up to $250K. The main emerging risk surface is the Soroban smart contract platform (launched 2024), which introduces a WASM execution environment that has received Trail of Bits scrutiny but is still maturing in production. Upgrade governance relies on validator quorum consensus rather than on-chain timelocks, which is appropriate for a federated L1 but means protocol changes depend on social coordination among node operators rather than transparent timelock delays.
sources: https://rekt.news (searched; no Stellar protocol-level entries found)
